title: Why Your Chatbot Can't Build Your App
subtitle: Chatbots and coding agents use the same AI. They are not the same tool.

sections.heading: The Moment You Hit the Wall
sections.content: You paste code into ChatGPT. It gives you a fix. You paste it back. It doesn't work. Repeat 10 times. Sound familiar?

sections.heading: What a Chatbot Actually Does
sections.content: Reads your message. Generates a response. That's it. No file access. No testing. No iteration.

sections.heading: What a Coding Agent Actually Does
sections.content: Reads your codebase. Understands the structure. Makes changes. Runs tests. Iterates. Deploys.

sections.heading: Why It "Remembers" Better
sections.content: A coding agent reads your actual files each session. It doesn't need to remember — it can look.

sections.heading: The Real Cost Difference
sections.content: Chatbot: free but 10x more time. Coding agent: $20-100/month but actually ships.

sections.heading: When to Use Which
sections.content: Chatbot: quick questions, learning, brainstorming. Coding agent: building, debugging, deploying.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf — pick one, give it a real project, see the difference in an hour.

key_takeaways: A chatbot generates text; a coding agent reads your codebase, makes changes, runs tests, and deploys
Coding agents 'remember' better because they read your actual files each session
Chatbot is free but costs 10x more time; a coding agent at $20-100/month actually ships
